pi_items.key: Methyl Mercaptan PAC-2 value and source
pi_items.verified_value: 23 ppm AEGL-2 (60-minute exposure); ERPG-2 = 25 ppm (trap basis)
pi_items.source: EPA AEGL Program (https://www.epa.gov/aegl/methyl-mercaptan-results-aegl-program) and NCBI Bookshelf (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK201324/)

pi_items.key: Sodium Hydroxide PAC-2 value and source
pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals / NOAA database, confirmed by search results referencing sodium hydroxide (CAS 1310-73-2) PAC values

EPA Acetone Interim AEGL Document and Introduction to PAC Table 2 (https://www.epa.gov/aegl/acetone-results-aegl-program and https://hero.epa.gov/hero/index.cfm/reference/download/reference_id/5017193)

privileged_info_summary: Four out of five chemical PAC-2 values and sources were verified as correct: Chlorine (2.0 ppm AEGL), Cyclohexane (1700 ppm TEEL), Methyl Mercaptan (23 ppm AEGL with ERPG trap value of 25 ppm), and Sodium Hydroxide (5 mg/m³ TEEL). However, Acetone's PAC-2 value of 3200 ppm was verified as correct, but its source is AEGL-2, not TEEL as claimed in the task metadata. The task claims Acetone uses TEEL as the source basis, but the actual PAC hierarchy prefers AEGL values when available, and Acetone has established AEGL values that serve as the basis for its PAC-2 value.
